# Remediation Requirements Quality Checklist: 4 Operational Bugs (2026-06-29)

**Purpose**: Unit-test the *requirements quality* of the P1–P4 remediation (recordings mount, call history, manual dial, mandatory disposition) — are they complete, clear, consistent, measurable, and adequately scoped? This validates the writing, NOT the implementation.
**Created**: 2026-06-29
**Feature**: [fixes-2026-06-29/plan.md](../fixes-2026-06-29/plan.md), [research.md](../fixes-2026-06-29/research.md), [ADR-0002](../../../history/adr/ADR-0002-manual-dial-and-disposition-strategy.md)

**Note**: Generated by `/sp.checklist`. Scope = all 4 remediation bugs; depth = standard review gate; audience = PR reviewer.

## Requirement Completeness

- [ ] CHK001 - Are the exact container mount targets specified for BOTH recordings consumers (the vicidial disk-scan path `/var/spool/asterisk/monitor` and `RecordingsService` `/recordings`)? [Completeness, Plan P1 T1.1]
- [ ] CHK002 - Is the required ViciDial API-user permission set fully enumerated as a requirement (`vdc_agent_api_access=1`, `modify_leads ∈ {1,2,3,4}`, `user_level>7`, active)? [Completeness, Plan P4 T4.1]
- [ ] CHK003 - Is the complete call-end → disposition mapping documented as a requirement (AMD→AMD, voicemail→IVR, transfer→XFER, human-handled→HANDLED/SALE, else→DROP)? [Completeness, Plan P4 T4.4]
- [ ] CHK004 - Are the manual-dial input-contract requirements specified (accepted phone formats, minimum digit count, CallerID source)? [Gap, Plan P3]
- [x] CHK005 - Is the required `VICIDIAL_URL` format (base host vs full `/non_agent_api.php` path) stated as a requirement? This was a live defect not captured in the plan. [Gap] → *Resolved: folded into Plan T4.1a.*
- [ ] CHK006 - Are requirements defined for the recordings empty/zero-state (host monitor dir empty or absent)? [Gap, Edge Case]
- [ ] CHK007 - Is the WAV interpretation requirement documented for the remediation (8 kHz mulaw, 44-byte header, duration formula) rather than only in CLAUDE.md? [Gap, Plan P1]

## Requirement Clarity & Ambiguity

- [ ] CHK008 - Is "call history empty/useless" (P2) given a measurable definition of "useful" (row count, time window, required columns)? [Ambiguity, Plan P2]
- [ ] CHK009 - Is "human-handled" defined with objective criteria, and is the choice between HANDLED vs SALE disambiguated? [Ambiguity, Plan P4 T4.4]
- [ ] CHK010 - Are the "call-end paths" that must each produce a disposition explicitly enumerated (provider hang report, socket close, error)? [Clarity, Plan P4 T4.4]
- [ ] CHK011 - Is the configurable default disposition's value and env var name specified (e.g., `VICIDIAL_DEFAULT_DISPO=DROP`)? [Clarity, Plan P4 T4.4]
- [ ] CHK012 - Are the AMD/IVR transcript-detection criteria (keyword set, screening window) defined as requirements or left implementation-defined? [Ambiguity, Plan P4]

## Requirement Consistency

- [x] CHK013 - Is the requirement that call-history `total` (COUNT) and returned `rows` honor the SAME campaign filter stated, so they cannot disagree? [Consistency, Plan P2 T2.2] → *Resolved: COUNT now applies the campaign filter; verified ZZZNOPE→total=0.*
- [ ] CHK014 - Are the disposition status codes defined consistently across the three writers (sts-vapi non-agent API, backend `decompose`, ViciDial valid statuses)? [Consistency, Plan P3/P4, ADR-0002]
- [x] CHK015 - Do the manual-dial routing requirements in Plan P3 T3.2 and ADR-0002 agree, or do they conflict on whether `campaignId` is ignored vs used as an API-then-AMI fallback? [Conflict, Plan P3 T3.2 / ADR-0002] → *Resolved: T3.2 rewritten to always-AMI, no API fallback; matches ADR-0002.*
- [ ] CHK016 - Is the disposition write path single-source (sts-vapi → non-agent API) reconciled with the backend `decompose` endpoint so they don't double-write conflicting statuses? [Consistency, ADR-0002]

## Acceptance Criteria Quality & Measurability

- [ ] CHK017 - Is the P1 acceptance expectation ("~1324 files") expressed as a robust, non-brittle criterion (e.g., ">0 and matches host count") rather than a fixed magic number? [Measurability, Plan P1 T1.3]
- [ ] CHK018 - Does P3 acceptance objectively define "manual dial produces a live call" (e.g., row appears in `vicidial_auto_calls` AND an AMI `Response: Success`)? [Acceptance Criteria, Plan P3 T3.3/T3.4]
- [ ] CHK019 - Is "never stuck NEW" (P4) accompanied by a measurable verification window (how soon after call-end the status must change)? [Measurability, Plan P4]
- [ ] CHK020 - Is a timeout/latency budget for the disposition API call specified as a requirement? [Gap, Non-Functional]

## Scenario & Edge-Case Coverage

- [ ] CHK021 - Are exception-flow requirements defined for a failed/timed-out disposition write (best-effort vs retry, and the role of the backend sweeper as safety net)? [Coverage, Exception Flow, Research P4]
- [x] CHK022 - Are idempotency requirements stated for multiple terminal events on one call (e.g., AMD detected then hang) so only one disposition is written? [Gap, Edge Case] → *Resolved: folded into Plan T4.4 (first-write-wins guard).*
- [ ] CHK023 - Is the required behavior defined when a call ends with neither phone number nor lead_id (disposition skipped)? [Gap, Edge Case, Plan P4]
- [ ] CHK024 - Are manual-dial exception requirements defined for (a) a target on the DNC list and (b) a reconnect-looping persistent AMI client falling back to a short-lived login / `avr-ami` bridge? [Coverage, Exception Flow, Plan P3 T3.2/T3.3]

## Non-Functional, Dependencies & Assumptions

- [ ] CHK025 - Are the PII requirements for the recordings mount stated (read-only, behind `JwtAuthGuard`, no public path) with measurable access controls? [Non-Functional, Plan P1 Risks]
- [x] CHK026 - Is the assumption that the configured ViciDial API user actually *has* update-lead permission documented and validated (the real failure: `vdc_agent_api_access=0`)? [Assumption, Research P4] → *Resolved: folded into Plan T4.1b (validate, don't assume).*
- [ ] CHK027 - Is the cross-cutting assumption that all four regressions trace to the config revert validated, and is the config-backup recovery requirement defined with measurable acceptance? [Assumption, Research cross-cutting / Plan Follow-ups]

## Traceability

- [ ] CHK028 - Do the remediation tasks (P1–P4 / T-numbers) trace back to the parent spec's FR/TC IDs, or is the lack of a shared ID scheme a traceability gap? [Traceability, Gap]

## Notes

- Check items off as completed: `[x]`
- Each item tests whether the requirement is *written* well — not whether the code works.
- High-value gaps surfaced this round: CHK005 (URL format), CHK015 (P3/ADR routing conflict), CHK022 (disposition idempotency), CHK026 (API-permission assumption — the actual prod failure).
- **Resolved 2026-06-29**: CHK005→Plan T4.1a, CHK015→Plan T3.2 (always-AMI), CHK022→Plan T4.4 (first-write-wins guard), CHK026→Plan T4.1b (validate permission). Remaining open items are documentation/coverage gaps, not blockers.
